samples.gvd:
module fsm #(
    parameter s0 = 3'b000,
    parameter s1 = 3'b001,
    parameter s2 = 3'b010,
    parameter s3 = 3'b011,
    parameter s4 = 3'b100,
    parameter s5 = 3'b101
) (
    input IN,
    output reg MATCH,
    input CLK,
    input RST
);

    reg [2:0] state, next_state;

    always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
        if (RST) begin
            state <= s0;
        end
        else begin
            state <= next_state;
        end
    end

    always @(*) begin
        case (state)
            s0: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state = s1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state = s0;
                end
            end
            s1: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state = s1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state = s2;
                end
            end
            s2: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state = s1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state = s3;
                end
            end
            s3: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state = s4;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state = s0;
                end
            end
            s4: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state = s5;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state = s0;
                end
            end
            s5: begin
                if (IN) begin
                    next_state = s1;
                end
                else begin
                    next_state = s2;
                end
            end
            default: begin
                next_state = s0;
            end
        endcase
    end

    always @(*) begin
        if (RST) begin
            MATCH = 1'b0;
        end
        else if (state == s4 && IN) begin
            MATCH = 1'b1;
        end
        else begin
            MATCH = 1'b0;
        end
    end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="fsm">
    <description>Mealy FSM detection circuit that detects a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, output MATCH is 1, and MATCH is 0 in other cases. Supports continuous input and loop detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to the FSM.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="S0">Initial state.</state>
        <state id="S1">State after detecting '1'.</state>
        <state id="S2">State after detecting '10'.</state>
        <state id="S3">State after detecting '100'.</state>
        <state id="S4">State after detecting '1001'.</state>
        <state id="S5">State after detecting '10011'.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="current_state" width_description="[2:0]">Register to hold the current state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" width_description="[2:0]">Register to hold the next state of the FSM.</reg>
        
        // State transition logic
        always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
            if (RST) begin
                current_state <= S0;
            end else begin
                current_state <= next_state;
            end
        end
        
        // Next state logic
        always @(*) begin
            case(current_state)
                S0: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S1;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end
                end
                
                S1: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S2;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S1;
                    end
                end
                
                S2: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S3;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S1;
                    end
                end
                
                S3: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S4;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end
                end
                
                S4: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                        next_state = S5;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S0;
                    end
                end
                
                S5: begin
                    if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                        next_state = S2;
                    end else begin
                        next_state = S1;
                    end
                end
                
                default: next_state = S0;
            endcase
        end
        
        // Output logic
        assign MATCH = (current_state == S5 && IN == 1'b1);
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>
        // Sequential logic block triggered on changes in IN, CLK, or RST
        always @(IN or CLK or RST) begin
            // Check for reset condition
            if(RST) begin
                MATCH <= 1'b0; // Set MATCH to 0 on reset
            end else begin
                // State machine logic
                case(current_state)
                    S0: begin
                        if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                            next_state <= S1; // Transition to S1 on IN=1
                        end else begin
                            next_state <= S0; // Stay in S0 on IN=0
                        end
                    end
                    
                    S1: begin
                        if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                            next_state <= S2; // Transition to S2 on IN=0
                        end else begin
                            next_state <= S1; // Stay in S1 on IN=1
                        end
                    end
                    
                    S2: begin
                        if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                            next_state <= S3; // Transition to S3 on IN=0
                        end else begin
                            next_state <= S1; // Stay in S1 on IN=1
                        end
                    end
                    
                    S3: begin
                        if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                            next_state <= S4; // Transition to S4 on IN=1
                        end else begin
                            next_state <= S0; // Go back to S0 on IN=0
                        end
                    end
                    
                    S4: begin
                        if(IN == 1'b1) begin
                            next_state <= S5; // Transition to S5 on IN=1
                        end else begin
                            next_state <= S0; // Go back to S0 on IN=0
                        end
                    end
                    
                    S5: begin
                        if(IN == 1'b0) begin
                            next_state <= S2; // Loop back to S2 on IN=0
                        end else begin
                            next_state <= S1; // Transition to S1 on IN=1
                        end
                    end
                    
                    default: next_state <= S0; // Default to S0
                endcase
                
                // Update MATCH based on current state
                if(next_state == S5 && IN == 1'b1) begin
                    MATCH <= 1'b1; // Set MATCH to 1 if in S5 and IN=1
                end else begin
                    MATCH <= 1'b0; // Otherwise, set MATCH to 0
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
